title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs handlers, middleware, dependencies, and application architecture from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the composed handler and dependency boundary. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for composed handler and dependency boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 2705 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
